title: Croatian Pork Tagine
description: A savory and cozy Croatian dish featuring pork cooked in a tagine, resulting in a jammy and aerated texture, perfect for a cold winter night, and is egg free friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the donabe over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the pork pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the paprika, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth and prunes, and bring to a boil.
Cover the donabe and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the donabe from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or thyme.
